name: Grid Layout Intent
summary: A primitive layout region-role (#1680 taxonomy): composition-intent = uniform cells, fit-to-container, intrinsically responsive. Identity is the arrangement, not the CSS — FUI ships one block per role and the mechanism (grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(min, 1fr))) is an impl seam. Project-less; per-project styling rides presentational variants + plateau assembler presets.

min-cell.description: The minimum cell inline-size floor that drives the auto-fit column count — the only responsive knob (no breakpoints). Smaller floor → more columns.

gap.description: Even spacing between cells in both axes, on a spacing scale.

equal-height.description: Whether cells in a row stretch to equal height. Default true.

description:
What This Is

Uniform responsive cells that fit as many columns as the container allows — each cell at least a minimum width — reflowing without media queries. Card galleries, product grids, dashboard tiles.

Composition-intent (the identity)

A primitive layout region-role (layoutTier: region-role), one of the per-role taxonomy ratified in #1680. Its identity is the composition-intent — uniform cells, fit-to-container, intrinsically responsive — impl-agnostic. FUI ships exactly one block for this role; the CSS mechanism (grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(min, 1fr))) is an impl seam, never the role's identity (platform-decisions.md#layout-role-composition-intent).

Dimensions (UX-only)

  • min-cell: The minimum cell inline-size floor that drives the auto-fit column count — the only responsive knob (no breakpoints). Smaller floor → more columns.
  • gap: Even spacing between cells in both axes, on a spacing scale (none · xs · sm · md · lg · xl).
  • equal-height: Whether cells in a row stretch to equal height. Default true (true · false).

Annotation, not identity

Stays distinct from masonry (shortest-column packing) — the two remain separate roles even after CSS masonry ships (the #1680 load-bearing case): uniform cells and ragged packing are different composition-intents.
